title: Add shared response-building utilities for canonical workflow guidance
summary: Introduce shared internal utilities that build standardized machine-readable success and failure responses for lifecycle-relevant MCP tools. This ticket establishes the implementation foundation for 0.5.4 by centralizing canonical workflow guidance generation so that lifecycle wrappers, verify/commit helpers, and state-facing tools can return consistent fields such as canonical status, canonical phase, next action, terminality, follow-up requirements, active transaction identity, and structured recovery guidance without duplicating logic across the codebase.
outputs: Shared internal helpers for building standardized lifecycle success responses
Shared internal helpers for building standardized lifecycle failure responses
A single implementation path for deriving canonical workflow guidance fields from canonical state
A reusable contract foundation that later tickets can apply to lifecycle wrappers and helper tools
acceptance_criteria: A shared implementation exists for constructing standardized workflow-guidance success responses.
A shared implementation exists for constructing standardized workflow-guidance failure responses.
The shared helpers can derive canonical guidance fields from canonical state rather than ad hoc caller-specific inference.
The shared helpers support terminality, next-action, follow-up, and active-transaction identity reporting in a consistent way.
The implementation reduces or eliminates duplicated response-shaping logic across lifecycle-aware tools.
The shared helpers are suitable for use by later tickets that normalize lifecycle wrapper and helper tool responses.
tasks: Design internal helper interfaces for standardized success and failure response construction based on the schema defined in p1-t01.

Centralize derivation of core workflow fields such as canonical status, canonical phase, next action, terminal flag, follow-up requirement, follow-up tool, active transaction id, and active ticket id.
Decide how contextual fields such as current step, verify status, commit status, integrity status, can-start-new-ticket, and resume-required should be incorporated into reusable builders.
Add or refactor implementation code so lifecycle-aware tools can call the shared builders instead of constructing workflow guidance ad hoc.
Preserve compatibility with existing payload fields where practical so later response normalization work can layer new canonical guidance onto current responses safely.
notes: This ticket is implementation-enabling infrastructure for 0.5.4 rather than a user-visible normalization pass by itself.
The goal is to make workflow guidance generation consistent before updating each lifecycle-aware tool to expose the full response contract.
The helpers should be built from canonical state and validated workflow semantics, not from client-side assumptions.
Where backward compatibility matters, the shared builders should make it easier to include both legacy fields and new canonical guidance fields during the transition.
